pattern: The minimum depth is the distance to the nearest leaf — and a node with only one child cannot count the missing side as depth zero, which is the trap.
hints: Depth here is measured to the closest leaf, a node with no children at all.
If a node is missing one child, you must descend into the side that exists rather than taking the empty side as depth one.
A breadth-first sweep finds the shallowest leaf first — return the level on which you meet a node with no children.

edge_inputs.description: balanced three-level tree, nearest leaf at depth 2
right-only chain — root is not a leaf, so depth follows the long single path
single node is its own leaf at depth 1
shallow leaf on the left while the right subtree runs deep

stress.note: first element non-null so the root exists
